title: cinnamomum rupestre
intro: Cinnamomum rupestre, also known as the mountain cinnamon, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Lauraceae. It is native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, and is often used to treat various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of Cinnamomum rupestre include decoctions, infusions, and essential oil, which can be used to make teas, ointments, and topical applications for its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
